rationale: The cited paper is used for a specific methodological precedent, not just generic background. In the data construction section, the authors explicitly state that, similar to Gielens (2012), they retain top-selling brands and aggregate the remainder into a 'rest brand' using defined share thresholds. This choice directly shapes the modeled choice set and empirical setup, so the citation has a clear functional role in how the study is implemented.
relation_description: The citing paper applies a data operationalization approach from Gielens (2012) to define which brands are included individually versus grouped into a residual brand category. This is a transfer of prior empirical setup conventions into the current promotion-calendar context.
